name: developer-docs-skills
description: A flat collection of ten codebase-neutral skills for writing and auditing the DOCUMENTATION of a multi-surface developer tool (CLI, SDK, REST API, MCP server, desktop/Electron dashboard, installer/upgrade, docs). Built on Diátaxis: every page is one of tutorial / how-to / reference / explanation, and mixing modes is the primary documentation failure. Ships docs-diataxis-audit (the flagship — classify every page, find mode-mixing and missing quadrants), the four generative mode-builders quickstart-builder (the install->first-call route, with a time-to-first-success target), tutorial-scaffold (learning-oriented, guaranteed success), how-to-recipe (task-oriented), and explanation-doc (understanding-oriented), reference-from-spec (machine-generated reference from an OpenAPI spec / --help dump / type signatures), and the read-only critics readme-audit (standard-readme + Make a README for a dev tool), doc-surface-drift (docs vs the actual CLI/API/SDK surfaces — partly machine-checkable), style-conformance (the Google developer documentation style guide), and code-sample-audit (runnable, copy-pasteable, idempotent, cross-language samples). Grounded in Diátaxis, the Google dev-docs style guide, Write the Docs / docs-as-code, and standard-readme. No agents, no shared docs — each skill stands alone and auto-routes on its own triggers. Completes the trio with experience-mapping-skills and dx-evaluation-skills: map the surfaces, evaluate them, then document them.
